response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer but lacks the hardware/silicon background this Principal Performance Architect role requires. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator work, deep neural network optimization, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design. Education is a BS in CS with no PhD/Master's, and while years of experience meet the bachelor's threshold, the experience is in web/mobile/backend rather than performance architecture for AI hardware.
strengths: 10+ years of software engineering experience meeting the bachelor's path year requirement
Some low-level/systems exposure via C/C++ at National Instruments and high-performance DLang microservices at Dunnhumby
Demonstrated ability to learn diverse stacks and contribute across embedded, backend, and tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep learning / DNN training or inference optimization background, and no GPU architecture work
No SIMD/MIMD architecture or chip-level performance analysis experience

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No DNN or GPU architecture optimization background
Sufficient years of general software experience
